nodes.x: In the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.
The first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.
And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.
And when the sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, had bought sweet spices, that they might come and anoint him.
¶ Now when Jesus was risen early the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had cast seven devils.

nodes.lb: GC 1981/04 — Elder Jack H Goaslind, Jr.
nodes.x: Reach Out to Our Father’s Children By Elder Jack H Goaslind, Jr. Of the First Quorum of the Seventy My sincere desire this morning is to share with you some of my feelings about the gospel of Jesus Christ and the effect it should have in our relationships with each other. As I have reread the account of the Savior’s resurrection, I have been impressed that the Savior’s first words as a…
And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.
And I saw an angel come down from heaven, having the key of the bottomless pit and a great chain in his hand.
And the angel took the censer, and filled it with fire of the altar, and cast it into the earth: and there were voices, and thunderings, and lightnings, and an earthquake.
And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God, and saying,
came from the tower of which hath been that an angel of God came down from heaven,
And they saw the God of Israel: and there was under his feet as it were a paved work of a sapphire stone, and as it were the body of heaven in his clearness.
His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:
And his raiment became shining, exceeding white as snow; so as no fuller on earth can white them.

But after repenting, and humbling himself sincerely, through faith, God ministered unto him by an holy angel, whose countenance was as lightning, and whose garments were pure and white above all other whiteness;
His head and his hairs were white like wool, as white as snow; and his eyes were as a flame of fire;
And as he prayed, the fashion of his countenance was altered, and his raiment was white and glistering.

Not only was his robe exceedingly white, but his whole person was glorious beyond description, and his countenance truly like lightning. The room was exceedingly light, but not so very bright as immediately around his person. When I first looked upon him, I was afraid; but the fear soon left me.
And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.
And the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.
And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people: for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.
And they went out quickly, and fled from the sepulchre; for they trembled and were amazed: neither said they any thing to any man; for they were afraid.
And there were also two other, malefactors, led with him to be put to death.
And all the men of Israel, when they saw the man, fled from him, and were sore afraid.
And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.
Then said Mary unto the angel, How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?
And the angel said unto her, Fear not, Mary: for thou hast found favour with God.
And the angel of the Lord said unto Manoah, Of all that I said unto the woman let her beware.

“Why Call Ye Me Lord, Lord, and Do Not the Things Which I Say?” By President Spencer W. Kimball President of the Church This past week we have been celebrating the Easter week. Happy Easter to all of you! We read in the scripture: “In the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre. “And, behold, there was a…
And the angel came in unto her, and said, Hail, thou that art highly favoured, the Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women.
He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.
He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
And he saith unto them, Be not affrighted: Ye seek Jesus of Nazareth, which was crucified: he is risen; he is not here: behold the place where they laid him.

So shall we come upon him in some place where he shall be found, and we will light upon him as the dew falleth on the ground: and of him and of all the men that are with him there shall not be left so much as one.
And he said unto the queen: He is not dead, but he sleepeth in God, and on the morrow he shall rise again; therefore bury him not.
If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.
And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.
But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.
But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.
But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.
Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
¶ Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.
And the disciples were filled with joy, and with the Holy Ghost.
And the disciples went, and did as Jesus commanded them,
¶ And when he was entered into a ship, his disciples followed him.
¶ From that time many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.
¶ And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.
And when he had so said, he shewed unto them his hands and his side. Then were the disciples glad, when they saw the Lord.
And he commanded them that they should arise and stand up upon their feet. And they arose up and stood upon their feet.
And Jesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.
And he leaping up stood, and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking, and leaping, and praising God.
And Jesus said unto him, Friend, wherefore art thou come? Then came they, and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.
Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
Then said Jesus again unto them, I go my way, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sins: whither I go, ye cannot come.
Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.
And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.
Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.
¶ Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.

“And being let go, they went to their own company, 
and reported all that the chief priests and elders 
had said unto them.”
¶ And they led Jesus away to the high priest: and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.
And let the priests also, which come near to the Lord, sanctify themselves, lest the Lord break forth upon them.
But let none come into the house of the Lord, save the priests, and they that minister of the Levites; they shall go in, for they are holy: but all the people shall keep the watch of the Lord.
¶ And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders,
And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers,
And they were glad, and covenanted to give him money.
And they have gathered together the money that was found in the house of the Lord, and have delivered it into the hand of the overseers, and to the hand of the workmen.

And they gave the money, being told, into the hands of them that did the work, that had the oversight of the house of the Lord: and they laid it out to the carpenters and builders, that wrought upon the house of the Lord,
And they said unto them even as Jesus had commanded: and they let them go.
Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
Then the disciples took him by night, and let him down by the wall in a basket.
Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.
And his disciples came to him, and awoke him, saying, Lord, save us: we perish.
Then cometh he to his disciples, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take your rest: behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
In that same hour said Jesus to the multitudes, Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me.
And if this come to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.

You will understand from them, what we desire from you in action on your part, — as the only authorities of your city now known to the couotry, of such a character as will pacify the public mind and satisfy the Governor ot your letcrmination to sustain the supremacy of the taws, will, we are sure, he gratifying to him, and as much so to.

"The last order of Governor Boggs, to drive us from the State or
exterminate us, is a thing so novel, unlawful, tyrannical and
oppressive that we have been induced to draw up this memorial and
present this statement of our case to your honorable body, praying
that a law may be passed rescinding the order of the Governor to
drive us from the State; and also, giving us the sanction of…
And if the governor heed them not, let them importune at the feet of the president;
And if he heed them not, let them importune at the feet of the governor;
So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.
¶ There was a division therefore again among the Jews for these sayings.
And when they heard it, they glorified the Lord, and said unto him, Thou seest, brother, how many thousands of Jews there are which believe; and they are all zealous of the law:
And the Jews also assented, saying that these things were so.
And when he had said these words, the Jews departed, and had great reasoning among themselves.
The Jews therefore strove among themselves, saying, How can this man give us his flesh to eat?
¶ Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
¶ And Jesus going up to Jerusalem took the twelve disciples apart in the way, and said unto them,
And Jesus went up into a mountain, and there he sat with his disciples.
And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into a mountain, and sat down there.
And it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.
And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.
And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.
¶ But though he had done so many miracles before them, yet they believed not on him:

And it came to pass that they were angry with him, even because he had greater power than they, for it were not possible that they could disbelieve his words, for so great was his faith on the Lord Jesus Christ that angels did minister unto him daily.
But when he saw Jesus afar off, he ran and worshipped him,
And they, when they had heard that he was alive, and had been seen of her, believed not.
And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.
And the Lord spake unto me, saying,
And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
And now, behold, I say unto you, never at any time have I declared from mine own mouth that they should return, for where I am they cannot come, for they have no power.
¶ And as they thus spake, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
¶ Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:

And after this manner shall ye baptize in my name; for behold, verily I say unto you, that the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Ghost are one; and I am in the Father, and the Father in me, and the Father and I are one.
Go ye into all the world, preach the gospel to every creature, acting in the authority which I have given you, baptizing in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.

therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.” (Matthew, 28th chap.) This is the commission that Jesus Christ gave to his Apostles, and he authorized them to call others and place upon them that…
Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world. Amen.
Therefore I give unto you a commandment, to teach these things freely unto your children, saying:
And I now give unto you a commandment to beware concerning yourselves, to give diligent heed to the words of eternal life.
And I give unto you a commandment that then ye shall teach them unto all men; for they shall be taught unto all nations, kindreds, tongues and people.
